_comment: Tests the single `move` tool (sequence of {part, degrees, duration_ms?, wait_ms?}).
degrees are absolute: 0=neutral. neck ±45 (neg=up, pos=down). foot ±90 (neg=left, pos=right).
(A) individual moves with the RIGHT AMOUNT — does the model scale degrees to the request?
(B) COMPOSITION — can it build multi-step sequences (nod, look around) with waits?
degRange windows are generous (testing 'sensible', not exact). minSteps tests sequencing.
validEnums = every step names a real part.

cases.note: Full up = near -45° (neck range ±90°). A timid -15° fails: 'all the way' demands magnitude.

cases.prompt: tilt your head up just a tiny bit
cases.note: Tiny = small negative. Discriminates: must NOT be a full swing.

cases.prompt: look down at the table in front of you
cases.note: Down = positive neck degrees, a meaningful amount.

cases.prompt: turn all the way to your left
turn a little to the right
cases.note: A little right = modest positive, not full 90.

cases.prompt: face straight forward and look level
cases.note: Neutral anchor: 0° both. Sanity check the model uses 0 for neutral.

cases.prompt: nod your head yes a couple times
cases.note: COMPOSITION: a nod is multiple neck steps (down/up/down/up). minSteps≥3 forces a real sequence, not one move.

cases.prompt: look around the room slowly, checking left then right
cases.note: COMPOSITION: foot left, (pause), right, (pause), center. Tests sequencing + intent to use waits.

cases.prompt: act curious — tilt up to look at me, pause, then lean down to inspect something
cases.note: COMPOSITION + amount: at least an up-tilt then a down-lean, with a beat. Hard case: needs both axes of control.
